frontmatter.title: Container Village
frontmatter.title_sub: Redefining Urban Living with Kampong Container
frontmatter.abstract: This project revolutionizes urban living in East Jakarta by employing an innovative housing approach using modular containers. Strategically placed near vital amenities, the development introduces two housing types, Type A and Type B, catering to different resident configurations. The inclusion of a plugin system boosts user mobility and facilitates an efficient, organic building process within the Kampong Container. Drawing inspiration from Indonesian culture, communal spaces on each floor cultivate a sense of community, encouraging self-economy and self-energy. Prioritizing sustainability and cultural values, the project addresses Jakarta’s housing requirements while reshaping contemporary living standards.
frontmatter.pr_contribution: Research, Concept, Design, BIM Modeling & Visualization
frontmatter.pr_contributor: Arnott Ferels, Cathleen Charity & Oliver Kenny
frontmatter.pr_jury: Arip Tirta, Dina Dellyana, Radhitya Trisya P, Aditya Prio S, M Basuki
frontmatter.pr_type: Collaborative work in the Professional Competition by Perumnas (National Urban Development Corporation)

mdx.body:
# Introduction An innovative housing project in [East Jakarta](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/East_Jakarta) repurposes a vacant plot initially designated for high-rises. Strategically located near TransJakarta bus stops, mosques, and schools, the site leverages Jakarta’s container accessibility. Challenges include ergonomic adjustments and affordable housing demand. Opportunities involve surplus containers, Gen Y and Z entering the market, and addressing wealth gaps. The project aims to meet Jakarta’s housing needs by capitalizing on container advantages and embracing the city’s unique characteristics. # Method: Unit Configuration ![Unit Configuration](./figures/container-village-method-unit-configuration.jpg "Unit Configuration") Unit Configuration. ![Physical model](./figures/container-village-physical-model.jpg "Physical model") Physical model. Architectural model illustrating the operational principles of the plugin system. # Development Phase ![Development Phase](./figures/container-village-development-phase.jpg "Development Phase") Development Phase. ![Plugin System](./figures/container-village-plugin-system.jpg "Plugin System") Plugin System. # Design
![The Exterior](./figures/container-village-pers-01.jpg "The Exterior") The Exterior. ![The Atrium](./figures/container-village-pers-02.jpg "The Atrium") The Atrium.
# Plan
![Level 1 (GF)](./figures/container-village-level-01.jpg "Level 1 (GF)") Level 1 (GF). ![Level 2 (Type B)](./figures/container-village-level-02.jpg "Level 2 (Type B)") Level 2 (Type B). ![Level 3 (Type B)](./figures/container-village-level-03.jpg "Level 3 (Type B)") Level 3 (Type B). ![Level 4 (Type B)](./figures/container-village-level-04.jpg "Level 4 (Type B)") Level 4 (Type B).
# Concept ## Self-economy ![Self-economy](./figures/container-village-concept-self-economy.jpg "Self-economy") Self-economy. The communal spaces on each floor deck and atrium, inspired by Indonesian culture, encourage a sense of togetherness and gathering. The Indonesian cultural characteristics of the market concept in the Kampong Container (Container Village) can also contribute to the local economy.
![Section A-A](./figures/container-village-section-aa.jpg "Section A-A") Section A-A. ![Section B-B](./figures/container-village-section-bb.jpg "Section B-B") Section B-B.
## Self-energy ![Illustration of self-energy generation through an axonometric drawing, detailing hierarchical levels from ground (GF) to rooftop (RT). Includes an in-depth axonometric drawing (zoom-in) of the rooftop floor, showcasing mechanisms such as solar panels, fish ponds, wind turbines, and vertical gardens.](./figures/container-village-concept-self-energy.jpg "Self-energy") Self-energy. The rooftop and decks of each floor are equipped with ecological independent energy sources such as vertical gardens, solar panels, fish ponds, and wind turbines.
## Self-installation ![Illustration of self-installation through a structural axonometric drawing, showcasing features such as elevator, rooftop floor, typical circulation on all floors, configuration of H-Beam 400×400mm in the structure, placement of stairs and elevator shafts, operation of container sockets, and placement of parking areas on level 1 (GF) elaborated in the building system of Kampung Kontainer (Container Village).](./figures/container-village-concept-self-installation.jpg "Self-installation") Self-installation. The plugin system, which allows for user mobility, contributes to the high efficiency and organic building process in the Kampung Kontainer (Container Village).
## Media 1. [https://www.instagram.com/p/B09xlxSn7SG/?igsh=MjFiN3lvOXpmc3Ni](https://www.instagram.com/p/B09xlxSn7SG/?igsh=MjFiN3lvOXpmc3Ni)
